metadata.title: One Logo Mark, Every Move Remotion Knows.
metadata.description: A technique-showcase reel animating the Musinique logo mark every way Remotion can — 16:9 landscape edition. One square SVG mark (2048x2048 viewBox, 73 subpaths), 20 techniques, for review: which moves deserve to live?

beats.narration_text: Hey, this is Liam, in for Bear. One logo mark. Twenty techniques. We are going to run the Musinique brand mark through every motion move Remotion knows, back to back, so you can judge which ones deserve to live.
Spring Entrance. The mark drops in from above on a standard spring. Watch the damping — is the settle clean or does it float?
Overshoot Spring. Same drop, but the spring config is looser — the mark squashes on landing and rebounds past center before settling. Squash and stretch, Remotion-style.
Draw-On Stroke. stroke-dashoffset traces the mark path first, then the fill floods in. The reveal follows the edge of the shape.
Mask Reveal. Two passes: first a horizontal wipe sweeps the mark into frame, then a radial iris opens from center. Same mark, two mask shapes.
Scale Zoom. Linear scale versus bezier scale — same endpoint, different feel. The bezier version has a brief ease-out overshoot. Compare the two halves.
Rotation. Full 360 degree spin, then a settle to zero. The mark is square, so every quarter turn lands on a recognizable orientation. Watch whether the logo survives the spin.
Skew and Shear. The mark leans left then right with a CSS skewX transform. At high angles this breaks the geometry — watch where the mark stops reading.
Opacity Through Blur. The mark fades in while simultaneously sharpening from a gaussian blur. Opacity and blur travel together — two levers, one reveal.
Color Interpolation. This is the treatment beat. Ink sweeps to terracotta and back, then to a deep teal and back. Outside normal brand law — the palette violation is intentional.
Kinetic Grid. The mark tiles across the full frame, then a staggered ripple rolls through the grid — each tile scales up in a wave and settles back. Pattern or noise?
Glitch Slices. Horizontal strips of the mark offset laterally, then snap clean. The resolution should feel like a correction. Decoration is the failure mode.
Trail Echo. The mark moves across frame leaving ghost copies behind it at decreasing opacity. Motion blur built from layers — not a filter but a composition.
Noise Wobble. Pseudo-random displacement on x and y axes driven by sinusoid pairs at offset frequencies. It shakes without a pattern. Live or nervous?
Elastic Physics. The mark drops, hits the floor hard, squashes flat on impact, then rebounds with decreasing oscillation. Spring mass is heavy here — the weight reads.
Card Flip. rotateY sweeps the mark through 180 degrees — mid-flip the fill color switches to terracotta, so you see a different face on each side. Perspective depth is set at 800.
Shadow Play. A box-shadow animates from zero to a deep directional drop shadow and back. Then a second pass with an ambient glow. Shadow as shape language.
Composer Summon. The mark builds in the Claude composer window — the prompt types, the spark fires terracotta, and the mark assembles as the result output. Ask then result.
Stroke Pulse. stroke-dashoffset cycles continuously — the mark outline pulses like a heartbeat, advancing and retreating. The fill stays solid underneath.
Scale Breathe. A very subtle continuous scale oscillation — barely perceptible at rest, obvious once you name it. The mark is alive without moving.

Exit Family. Three exits back to back: shrink-spin to nothing, blur-out to white, mask-close radial iris. The mark leaves three ways. Which one you would actually cut to black on — that is the judgment.
Now you have the receipts. Take this prompt, run it on your own SVG mark, and flag the two or three moves that actually serve the brand.
